question: Will CPI rise more than 1.1% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has been officially released showing a 0.3% monthly increase on a seasonally adjusted basis. This is significantly below the 1.1% threshold required for YES resolution. Multiple sources confirm this figure from the Bureau of Labor Statistics, making the outcome definitively NO. The market is severely mispriced at 4%, likely due to confusion or traders not yet processing the official data release.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March CPI increased only 0.3% monthly
Market closes tomorrow but outcome is already determined by released data
Resolution criteria requires single-decimal precision - 0.3% is far below 1.1%
risks: Potential data revision (though highly unlikely for such a large magnitude)
Misunderstanding of resolution criteria (though seems straightforward)
